passages.passage_text:
Directions (Q. Nos. 14-15) Read the following information carefully and answer the questions given below. [PNB (PO) 2010]
I. 'P × Q' means 'P is sister of Q'.
II. 'P + Q' means 'P is mother of Q'.
III. 'P - Q' means 'P is father of Q'.
IV. 'P ÷ Q' means 'P is brother of Q'.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If P $ Q means 'P is the father of Q, P # Q means 'P is the mother of Q, P * Q means P is the sister of Q. Then, how is Q related to N in N # L $ P * Q?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following represents 'M is the nephew of R'?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following represents 'W is grandfather of H'?

solutions.solution: By decoding given information with symbols of family diagram, we get family diagrams showing (P $ Q) = Father relationship, (P # Q) = Mother relationship, (P * Q) = Sister relationship. Now, by applying above decoding method, We draw the following family diagram (N # L $ P * Q). The diagram shows N as Mother, then Father relationship, then P and Q as sisters. Clearly, the gender of Q cannot be determined from the given information and hence, Q can either be grandson or granddaughter of N. Therefore, we can say that data is inadequate to establish a relationship between N and Q.

By decoding given information with symbols of family diagram, we get family relationship symbols showing (P × Q) = Sister relationship, (P + Q) = Mother relationship, (P - Q) = Father relationship, (P ÷ Q) = Brother relationship.
